h1.en: Free Online File Tools for Students

description.en: Free tools to help students convert images, compress PDFs, and handle common file format tasks for school and university assignments.

intro.en: Handle every file format task you'll encounter at school or university. Convert images for presentations, compress large PDFs to meet email size limits, merge assignment files, and format code — all for free, no account required.

en: Compress PDFs to fit email attachment limits
Convert images to JPG for smaller presentation file sizes
Merge multiple documents into one PDF
Format and validate JSON for coding assignments
Resize images to meet exact dimension requirements

question.en: Are these tools really free for students?

answer.en: Yes. All tools are completely free — no account, no subscription, no watermarks. Every tool runs in your browser.

question.en: Can I use these tools on my school computer?

answer.en: Yes. They run in any modern browser without software installation. No admin rights required.

question.en: Do these tools work offline in the library?

answer.en: After the page first loads, most tools work without an internet connection. Image and PDF tools are fully offline-capable.

description.en: Convert, compress, and optimize images in the browser. Essential free tools for graphic designers, UI/UX designers, and illustrators.

intro.en: Designers need format flexibility. Convert between PNG, JPG, WebP, AVIF, and SVG. Compress images for web delivery. Resize for social media specs. Add watermarks to protect your work. All in the browser — no Photoshop needed.

en: Convert between all major image formats in seconds
Compress images for web without visible quality loss
Resize images to exact pixel dimensions for any platform
Add watermarks to protect your designs
Convert to modern formats like WebP and AVIF for web performance

question.en: Can I convert SVG to PNG in the browser?

answer.en: Yes. Our SVG to PNG converter renders vector SVG files to raster PNG at any resolution you choose.

question.en: Can I compress images for web without quality loss?

answer.en: Yes. Our image compressor uses smart compression that reduces file size while maintaining visual quality.

question.en: Do these tools support batch processing?

answer.en: Yes. Most image tools support uploading and processing multiple files simultaneously.

description.en: Format JSON, encode Base64, validate YAML, generate hashes, decode JWTs, and more. Privacy-first developer utilities — nothing sent to a server.

intro.en: A complete browser-based toolkit for developers. Format and validate JSON, YAML, and XML. Encode and decode Base64, URLs, and HTML entities. Generate cryptographic hashes. Inspect JWTs. Test regular expressions. All processing is local — safe for sensitive data.

en: Format and validate JSON, YAML, and XML instantly
Encode and decode Base64, URLs, and HTML entities
Generate MD5, SHA-1, SHA-256, and SHA-512 hashes
Decode and inspect JWT tokens safely
Convert between JSON, YAML, XML, and CSV formats
All processing is local — safe for API keys and credentials

question.en: Are these developer tools safe for sensitive data?

answer.en: Yes. All processing runs in your browser. No data is sent to any server. Safe for JWT tokens, API keys, and confidential JSON.

question.en: Can I use these tools via command line?

answer.en: These are browser-based tools. For CLI use, consider jq (JSON), openssl (hashing), or base64 system utilities.
Yes. After the page loads, all tools work without an internet connection.

Merge multiple reports into a single PDF

question.en: Is it safe to use these tools for confidential business documents?

answer.en: Yes. All processing happens in the browser. Documents are never uploaded to any server. This makes them safer than cloud-based alternatives for confidential files.

question.en: Can multiple employees use these tools?

answer.en: Yes. The tools are available to anyone with a browser, with no seat limits or accounts required.

h1.en: Free Image & File Tools for Bloggers

description.en: Optimize images for your blog, convert to WebP for speed, compress for fast loading, and resize to fit your theme — all free, all in the browser.

intro.en: Fast-loading images improve SEO and user experience. Convert images to WebP or AVIF for maximum compression. Resize to your exact theme dimensions. Compress without quality loss. Add watermarks to protect original content.

en: Convert images to WebP for 30% smaller file sizes
Resize to exact blog theme dimensions
Compress JPG and PNG without visible quality loss
Add watermarks to protect original blog images
Convert screenshots to web-optimized formats

question.en: What image format should I use for my blog?

answer.en: WebP offers the best compression for blog images with broad browser support. AVIF is even smaller but has less support. JPG is safe for photographs; PNG for screenshots and graphics.

question.en: How can I speed up my blog by optimizing images?

answer.en: Convert images to WebP, compress them to under 100KB where possible, and resize to your exact display dimensions. This can cut page load time significantly.

h1.en: Free Image & PDF Tools for Ecommerce Sellers

description.en: Optimize product images, compress for fast page loads, and prepare PDFs for online stores. Free browser tools for Shopify, WooCommerce, and Etsy sellers.

intro.en: Fast product pages convert better. Compress and optimize product images without losing quality. Convert to WebP for maximum speed. Resize to marketplace-specific dimensions (Amazon, Shopify, Etsy). Process multiple product images at once with batch tools.

en: Compress product images for faster page load speeds
Resize to exact marketplace dimension requirements
Convert to WebP for 30–50% smaller file sizes
Batch process hundreds of product images
Add watermarks to protect product photography
Prepare PDF product catalogs and spec sheets

question.en: What image size should I use for ecommerce product photos?

answer.en: Most platforms recommend 800×800 to 2000×2000 pixels. Shopify recommends 2048×2048. Keep file sizes under 500KB for fast loading.

question.en: Should I use JPG or WebP for product images?

answer.en: WebP is better — it's 25–35% smaller than JPG at the same quality. All major browsers support WebP. Use our JPG to WebP converter to switch your existing images.

question.en: Can I compress a PDF to share on Google Classroom?

answer.en: Yes. Our PDF compressor reduces file size significantly while maintaining readability. Compressed PDFs are easier to share via email or upload to Google Classroom.

question.en: How do I convert a PDF to an image for a presentation?

answer.en: Use our PDF to Images tool. It converts each PDF page to a PNG or JPG that you can insert directly into PowerPoint or Google Slides.
